introduction: The Signals and Systems Lab offers an interactive platform to explore core concepts in signal representation and orthogonality, system analysis, and signal transformation. It bridges theoretical foundations with practical applications, supporting students in mastering complex system behaviors.

experiments.name: LTI system analysis: impulse response and step response
LTI system analysis: magnitude and phase response

objective:
• Building Core Understanding: Visualize abstract concepts like Fourier series, Laplace, and Z-transforms through intuitive simulations.
  • Connecting Theory to Practice: Analyze real-world signals and systems, exploring stability, frequency responses, and filter behaviors.
  • Fostering Interactive Learning: Experiment with signal synthesis, system responses, and transformations, strengthening problem-solving skills.

courseAlignment.description: Signals and Systems is a professional core course in Electronics and Communication Engineering
universities: (PCC-EC05) recommended in the AICTE model curriculum
